supplies.title: Crayola Model Magic Dough
supplies.rationale:
- Geography: Used for making models and relief maps in Terms 2 & 3.
- Science: For making a model of a pond habitat in Term 1.
supplies.note: Replenish as needed.
supplies.maySub: clay, Play-Doh, or homemade salt dough. (See lesson plans for recipe.)
supplies.qty: 1 pack would be enough for 2 Geography models (This contains 4 packs.)
supplies.usedInText:
Geography: Grade 1
Geography: Grades 4-6
Geography: Grades 7-8 (U.S.)
Geography: Grades 7-8 (Canada)
General Science: Grade 1

supplies.title: Globe
supplies.rationale: Used for many Geography lessons in grades 1 and 2. Only used a few times in grade 3.
supplies.note: Inflatable globes are widely available and are a great option when money or space is tight.
supplies.maySub: any globe, even inflatable one.

Geography: Grade 1
Geography: Grade 2 (U.S.)
Geography: Grade 2 (Canada)

supplies.title: Household Items - Geography: Grade 1
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- An orange (you'll need one a couple of separate times) (T1)
- Small object like car, lego figure, action figure, doll (T1)
- Ball for student to try to spin on finger (optional) (T1)
- Lamp, flashlight, light (T1)
- Blocks or Legos to build a model of a room (T1/2)
- Penny or other small object (T1)
- Small sticker or sticky note (T1)
- Object like stuffed animal, toy, kitchen utensil, brush, something that can be hidden and found. Will be used outside in one lesson. (T1) 
- Balloon, basic round latex (T1)
- Paved area (T2)
- Treat like might be found in a treasure hunt (snack, small toy, or gift of some kind) (T2)
- Pan or tray that will hold water (T3)
- Pot or tea kettle for boiling water (T3)
- Jar for measuring rainfall. Wide mouth is best. (T3) 
- Long art paper or a few pieces of paper taped together end to end (T3)
- Glue or tape (T3)

supplies.title: Household Items - Geography: Grade 2
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- something large and flat like a cookie sheet or cutting board (T2)
- clay (Model Magic or homemade recipe is at end of Geography lessons in appendix) (T2)
- wide mouth jar (T2)
- pen or Sharpie (T2)
- (gallon bag, marker, blue food color, tape) OR (plastic wrap, clear large and small bowls) (T2)
- wooden blocks, Duplos or Legos made into blocks, small boxes (like the size of single-serving cereal boxes), or small books approximately 3x5 inches (T2)
- large piece of paper (art paper, legal pad paper, larger than printer paper) (T3)

supplies.title: Household Items - Geography: Grade 3
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Clay (Model Magic or homemade recipe is at end of Geography lessons in appendix) (T1/2)
- Dental floss (T1) 
- A sturdy base to trace an outline map and build a model on (cardstock, cardboard, etc)(T1/2)
- Tool to carve clay with (toothpick, kitchen knife, clay modeling tools, etc.) (T1/2)
- Two pieces of cork or some other floating object (T1)
- A timer with seconds (phone is fine) (T1)
- Street map of your city. Can often find online and print off. (T2)
- Watering can or something to pour water slowly and gently (T2)
- Shallow pan like cake pan or cookie sheet with sides. (T3)
- Rock or other large heavy item that is deeper than your shallow pan. Could use an inverted bowl. (T3)
supplies.usedInText:
Geography: Grade 3 (U.S.)
Geography: Grade 3 (Canada)

- toothpicks or pins (T1/2/3)

supplies.rationale:
- Geography: Used for making models and relief maps in Terms 2 & 3.
- Science: For making a model of a pond habitat in Term 1.
supplies.note: Replenish as needed.
supplies.maySub: clay, Play-Doh, or homemade salt dough. (See lesson plans for recipe.)
supplies.qty: 1 pack would be enough for 2 Geography models (This contains 4 packs.)
supplies.usedInText:

supplies.title: Black Pen
supplies.rationale: used for drawing maps.
supplies.maySub: any black pen that won't bleed.

supplies.title: Protractor
supplies.rationale: Used in some Mapping the World with Art lessons.
supplies.maySub: any protractor. There is a paper one in the book you can print on cardstock and cut out if you don't have one.
